chunks.text: Seth R. Sanders is Professor Emeritus in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. His contact information includes 518 Cory Hall, phone 510-642-4425, and email sanders@eecs.berkeley.edu. He has a personal homepage, publications list, and research support information available. Administrative support is provided by Monica Naputi at mnaputi@berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: What is Seth Sanders' position and contact information?

chunks.text: Seth Sanders received S.B. degrees in Electrical Engineering and Physics, and S.M. and Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from MIT in 1981, 1985, and 1989 respectively. He worked as a Design Engineer at Honeywell Test Instruments Division before joining UC Berkeley's faculty in 1989. He received the NSF Young Investigator Award in 1993 and IEEE Best Paper Awards, and has served in IEEE leadership roles including Chair of the Technical Committee on Computers in Power Electronics.
chunks.question: What are Seth Sanders' educational background and career highlights?
chunks.answer: MIT degrees and UC Berkeley faculty since 1989.

chunks.text: Seth Sanders' research areas include Power and Energy (ENE), Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR), and Integrated Circuits (INC), with a focus on power and electronics systems. He is affiliated with the Berkeley Power and Energy Center (BPEC). His selected publications cover topics like grid-tied AC-DC conversion, DC microgrids, buck converter ICs, flywheel energy storage, digital PWM converters, microfabricated inductors, and power conversion averaging methods.
chunks.question: What are Seth Sanders' main research areas and centers?
chunks.answer: Power electronics and Berkeley Power and Energy Center.

chunks.text: Seth Sanders has received significant honors including being named an IEEE Fellow in 2009 and receiving the NSF Young Investigator Award in 1993. His publications demonstrate contributions to power electronics, with papers in IEEE journals on topics such as converter design, energy storage systems, and control methods for power conversion circuits.
chunks.question: What major honors has Seth Sanders received?
chunks.answer: IEEE Fellow and NSF Young Investigator Award.
